senses.gloss: to burn, to hurt, to sting
to burn, to destroy by fire, to singe, to scorch

examples.text: قَالُوا حَرِّقُوهُ وَٱنْصُرُوا آلِهَتَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ فَاعِلِينَ
examples.english: They said, "Burn him and support your gods - if you are to act."

senses.gloss: spoiler (document, review or comment that discloses the ending or some key surprise or twist in a story)

etymology.text:
From the root ح ر ق (ḥ r q), to burn.

Intensive of حَرَقَ (ḥaraqa, “to burn”).
